name: multichat-personnel
description: Internal-employee personnel — owns /v1/employees/* CRUD + raw-MD edit routes, the in-memory employee map, and the on-disk bridge/employees/<id>.md persistence. Seeds the standard `ceodave` employee on first boot. Registers an `employee` entry in the `participantKinds` register so the multichat invitee picker (and any future register-based participant resolver) sees employees only when this module is active. Data dir bridge/employees/ stays at bridge root — user state, not module state. Disabling this module 404s the /v1/employees/* routes; the FE participant picker should fall back to '[unloaded participant]' for any session that still references an employee id (deferred to a follow-up FE task).
